steps.prompt: Which word is a relative pronoun?

steps.reveal: qui = who (nom. masc. sg., subject of 'es')

hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'quo' — 'qui' is nominative, subject of 'es'

hints.to eat: That would be 'edere' — 'es' here is from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to go out: That would be 'exire' — 'es' is from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to have: That would be 'habere' — 'es' is from 'esse' (to be)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sis' — 'es' is present indicative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'fuisti' — 'es' is present indicative
hints.imperfect indicative: Imperfect would be 'eras' — 'es' is present indicative

hints.edo, esse, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat)
hints.habeo, habere, habui, habitum: These are the parts of 'habere' (to have)
hints.exsto, exstare, exstiti, —: These are the parts of 'exstare' (to stand out)

steps.reveal: Pater = Father (voc., direct address in prayer)

hints.nominative: While 'Pater' looks identical in nominative, this is a prayer addressing God — vocative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'patris' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'patris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'patris' ends in -is

steps.reveal: caelis = in the heavens (abl. pl., with preposition 'in')

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'caeli' — 'caelis' is dative/ablative plural
hints.dative: While 'caelis' could be dative, after 'in' (location) it is ablative
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'caelorum' — 'caelis' is ablative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'caeli' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'caeli' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'caeli' ends in -i

translation: Our Father, who art in heaven
